reasoning.typography: Default system fonts, no typographic personality. Readable but utterly generic — this could be any Octopress/Hugo blog from 2012.
reasoning.hierarchy: Weak headline differentiation. The orange header dominates but content below is flat chronological lists with minimal visual weight variation.
reasoning.distinctiveness: The diagonal stripe pattern and orange are unique enough, but the body content structure (chronological date-title lists) is pure blog stencil. The voice in post titles saves it.
reasoning.color: Orange + gray + white is restrained and disciplined. Orange used sparingly as accent in stripes and links. Could be more boring but works.
reasoning.density: Good content density — no afraid-of-text syndrome. Lists are tight, readable, functional. Not trying to be a landing page, actually has substance.
reasoning.voice: Structure genuinely reflects the person: 'here's everything I've written, organized how I organize it.' No fake 'Selected Works' curation. Honest blog archive energy.

winning_patterns: Unapologetically dense — shows all posts, no fake minimalism hiding content
Diagonal stripe motif creates memorable visual identity despite generic typography
Navigation is functional and honest: 'Favorites/TIL/Zines' reflects actual content types
Post titles have personality ('tcpdump is amazing', 'the fish shell is awesome') — not SEO slop
anti_patterns: Typography has zero character — default sans-serif could be any blog platform
Header hierarchy is weak — name, nav, and body content all compete at similar weights
Orange header box feels disconnected from content below — two different design systems
Date-title lists lack visual rhythm — every row has identical weight
